variants.llm_generated: Thank you for your question. Fatty liver, especially at grade 2, is quite common nowadays due to lifestyle and dietary habits. The good news is that it can often be managed with some changes. I recommend adopting a healthier diet by reducing your intake of oily, fatty, and high-carbohydrate foods. Increasing your physical activity with regular exercise is also very important. Please avoid alcohol, as it can worsen fatty liver. It would be helpful to check your cholesterol and blood sugar levels as well. If these are significantly elevated, your doctor may consider starting medication. For now, focus on lifestyle modifications and follow up with your healthcare provider for further guidance.
variants.llm_humanized: Hey, thanks for reaching out. Fatty liver is actually pretty common these days, mostly because of our diets and how much time we spend sitting. The good thing is, you can do a lot to help it. Try cutting back on oily and fatty foods, and maybe skip the carbs when you can. Getting into a regular exercise routine really helps too. Also, it’s best to avoid alcohol. You might want to get your cholesterol and blood sugar checked, just to see where you stand. If those numbers are high, your doctor might talk to you about medication. But for now, focus on eating better and moving more, and keep in touch with your doctor.
variants.human_polished: Hi, I reviewed your ultrasound report and understand your concern about fatty liver. There's no need to worry too much—fatty liver is very common these days, mainly due to our current eating habits and sedentary lifestyles. Fatty liver means there is excess cholesterol deposited in the liver, but it is still reversible. You should make some dietary changes by reducing oily, fatty, and high-carbohydrate foods, and increase your physical activity with regular exercise. Please also check your serum cholesterol levels and let me know the results. If your cholesterol is very high, we can consider starting medication. Take care, and remember to avoid alcohol. Recommended tests: Fasting Lipid Profile, FBS, PPBS. Follow a fatty liver diet and exercise regularly.
variants.dictated_spoken: Hi so I looked at your ultrasound report and I get that you’re worried about your fatty liver but honestly you don’t need to stress too much about it it’s really common these days because of our food habits and not moving around much with all the digital stuff we do fatty liver just means there’s extra cholesterol in your liver but it can be fixed you just need to change your diet cut down on oily and fatty foods and carbs and try to exercise more regularly that really helps also check your cholesterol levels and let me know what they are if they’re really high we can think about starting some medicine for now just focus on diet and exercise and avoid alcohol okay also maybe get a fasting lipid profile and blood sugar tests done take care
